change_tracking.purpose: Track whether manual Google Ads changes are working by comparing normalized last-7-days performance against last-month baseline on each refresh.
change_tracking.current_connector_limit: The connected Google Ads tool can read reports and upload limited conversion/audience data, but it does not currently expose direct campaign, budget, bid, ad, or negative-keyword mutation tools or change-history reports.
status_rules: Working: CPA down 10%+ or conversion rate up while conversion volume holds steady.
Worsening: spend/day is up and CPA is up 25%+, or spend continues with zero conversions.
Noisy: fewer than 20 clicks or less than $50 recent spend; wait for more data before major action.
change_tracking.approval_rule: If future Google Ads mutation-capable tools are connected, require explicit approval of the exact proposed edits before writing changes back.
change_tracking.recommendation_completion_rule: Do not remove action cards just because a change was made. Keep them until the stated success metric is met or a human marks them complete.
daily_operator_sequence: Review P0 queue and contain spend first.
Run search-term negative review for each P0/P1 campaign.
Apply budget/bid adjustments manually in Google Ads.
Log the exact change and expected result.
Use the next daily 7d-vs-last-month refresh to decide whether to keep, reverse, or scale the change.

google_ads_insights.daily_update_note: Daily refresh should pull current last-30-days, last-7-days, and last-month windows, rebuild this action queue, and then publish the sanitized dashboard snapshot.
operator_review_order: P0 today: pause/cut spend first, then inspect the listed search-term or PMax theme before re-scaling.
P1 this week: reduce bid/budget pressure, add the listed negative themes, and tighten the named structure issue.
P2 monitor/scale: verify lead quality first; scale only 10-15% if CPA and conversion pace hold.
Log each manual change with office, campaign, action, and success metric so tomorrow’s trend can judge it.
google_ads_insights.recommendation_detail_note: Short version: each action now gives one campaign-specific move, one inspection focus, one negative-keyword focus, one structure fix, and one success metric. Keep cards visible until the next refresh proves the change worked or a human marks it complete.
dashboard_priorities: Review high-risk campaigns first: spend with zero or low conversions, especially high-CPC search and thin-conversion PMax.
Protect efficient AI DNM, Spanish, Dentical/insurance, wisdom teeth, and emergency campaigns only where lead quality is confirmed.
Use the public dashboard for sanitized decisions; keep raw IDs, query details, and account mapping private.

_sanitization.policy: Public dashboard exposes only aggregate metrics, category/status summaries, redacted operator action items, operating guardrails, and aggregated Google Ads campaign and ad-group metrics. Individual prospect emails, prospect names, reply sender names/addresses, free-text reply summaries, the operations Google Sheet ID and URL, recipient-level batch details, private operations-repo commit hashes, and Google Ads manager/customer account identifiers are removed.
removed_fields: next_actions free-text references to specific prospects
daily[].next_focus references to specific prospect names
human_followups[] redacted to action and channel only; identities live in private Sheet
google_ads_insights account_label raw text (replaced with mapping-pending placeholder)
b2b_reply_detail timeline excludes sender names, emails, organizations, phone numbers, and raw reply text
google_ads_keyword_focus is keyword-ideas API output only; no customer/manager IDs, no search-term reports

b2b_reply_detail.title: B2B reply detail
b2b_reply_detail.privacy_note: Public view is sanitized: no sender names, emails, organizations, phone numbers, or raw reply text.

reply_timeline.suggested_next_action_public: Monitor for the forwarded contact; if no response, send a light follow-up.
Recycle in roughly 90 days with a note referencing their timing constraint.

reply_timeline.public_theme: Community channel suggested alternate referral path
reply_timeline.suggested_next_action_public: Consider OASIS/older-adult program call; avoid endorsement ask unless paid advertising is desired.
Suppress address and source alternate public contact before any future outreach.
Monitor and send light follow-up after 5 business days if no reply.
Prepare human-approved one-page senior oral-health resource and send as follow-up.

what_is_working: Senior/caregiver and caregiver-resource channels produced the clearest positive signal.
Fitness/wellness outreach produced a warm partnership-review signal worth a follow-up.
Community/faith channels can surface referral paths, but endorsement asks are weaker than program-specific older-adult channels.
next_copy_tests: Lead senior/caregiver copy with a concrete one-page oral-health resource plus optional short educational session.
For wellness/fitness, frame as Dental Wellness 101 for members, not a generic partnership ask.
For community organizations, ask for the correct older-adult program contact instead of endorsement.
